The '''Agee''' were a tiny, winged, [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] that came in two varieties: those capable of flight and those not. In [[17 ABY/Legends|17 ABY]], several Agee resided on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]]. [[Unidentified Agee patron|One such]] Agee was almost trapped in the [[Crystal Jewel]] [[Casino/Legends|casino]] when it started to collapse following the [[Bombing of the Senate Hall]]. [[Unidentified Agee well-wisher|Another Agee]] went to visit [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] following her ascension to [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] after incumbent [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] resigned to rescue her brother, [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]; however, the Agee left before she could meet with Mothma.

==Biology and appearance==
The Agee were a tiny,<ref name="CSWE" /> [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]], [[Species/Legends|species]] that had wings.<ref name="The New Rebellion" /> The species existed in two varieties; one capable of independent flight and the other flightless. The latter variety was significantly more delicate than the former.<ref name="CSWE" />

==History==
{{Quote|We're underground, pal. If we don't get out now, we might not get out at all.|Han Solo comments on the collapse of the Crystal Jewel|<ref name="The New Rebellion">''[[The New Rebellion]]''</ref>}}
In [[17 ABY/Legends|17 ABY]], several Agee resided on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] and then under [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] control. That [[Standard year/Legends|year]], an [[Unidentified Agee patron|Agee]] was present in the [[Crystal Jewel]], an underground [[Casino/Legends|casino]], when [[Bombing of the Senate Hall|explosions]] rocked the nearby [[Senate Hall]]. As the shockwave from the explosion threatened to collapse the casino, the Agee was trapped on a crumbling section of the ceiling until helped down by a fellow patron, [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]. Solo also shot the lock on the casino door, allowing its patrons to vacate the unstable premises.<ref name="The New Rebellion" />

The explosions were caused by [[darksider]] [[Kueller]], who kidnapped [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], holding him hostage in a gambit to force Skywalker's sister and then [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]], [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]], to transfer presidency of the New Republic to him. Organa Solo, however, resigned her position, passing it to [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]], before leaving Coruscant to rescue her brother. Following these events, many individuals visited Mothma's anteroom, hoping to convey to her their well-wishes. These individuals included a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Unidentified Agee well-wisher|Agee]] that decided to visit Mothma on a lark. Mon Mothma's [[Mon Mothma's receptionist droid|receptionist droid]] placed the Agee ahead of the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]], who were waiting to convey the findings of their investigation into the Senate Hall bombing to the Chief of State. When R2-D2 made a shrill noise in order to prevent C-3PO from revealing their findings—that the bombing had been caused by [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] detonators—the Agee quickly flew out of the room.<ref name="The New Rebellion" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[Kristine Kathryn Rusch]] created the Agee species for her novel ''[[The New Rebellion]]'', published by [[Bantam Spectra]] in [[1996]]. They were subsequently mentioned in the ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ([[1998]]) and the ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ([[2008]]). The latter source introduces new material that appears to be a [[Retroactive continuity|retcon]] explaining why ''The New Rebellion'' portrays an Agee as trapped on a ceiling when it later describes the species as capable of flight. ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'' handles this by creating two varieties of Agee, which is calls "flightless and winged" despite both Agee in the novel possessing wings.<ref name="CUSWE">This description was originally found in The Completely Unofficial Star Wars Encyclopedia, maintained by [[Bob Vitas]], a contributor to ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia''.</ref>
